title: MfaMethodsSummary — response shape samples
description: Illustrative response shapes for GET /api/v1/me/mfa. Real values are ULIDs / timestamps / integers — the samples fix the shape. This is the primary summary DTO that drives every SPA rendering decision on the MFA panel.

example_fully_enrolled.note: Response shape when the caller has TOTP + multiple WebAuthn credentials + full recovery codes.

example_totp_only.note: Response shape when the caller has TOTP but no WebAuthn credentials.

example_low_codes.note: Response shape when the caller has been consuming recovery codes and is now at the low-water threshold.

example_step_up_stale.note: Response shape when the caller's last MFA challenge is stale + they hold entitlements to step-up-gated actions.

example_not_enrolled.note: Response shape when the caller has zero MFA factors. The `step_up_required_for` array is empty because step-up is undefined without a primary factor. The SPA should render the enrolment CTA prominently.

notes: The endpoint is idempotent + cache-friendly (private, max-age=30 — matches the mfa:posture cache TTL). Repeated calls in the same request return the cached response.

`recovery_codes_low` is a boolean derived at response-projection time from `recovery_codes_remaining < config('mfa.recovery_codes.low_water_threshold')` — it exists so the SPA doesn't need to know the threshold value.

`step_up_required_for` is populated by evaluating the caller's entitlements against `config('mfa.step_up.gated_actions')` — an entitled action appears in the array only when the last MFA challenge is older than `mfa.step_up.sensitive_action_ttl_minutes`.
None of these fields carry credential material — the endpoint is safe under the tenant's default (non-PII-elevated) permission set.
